wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Code-To-UML project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a browser-first UML documentation generator that transforms code analysis reports into interactive HTML documents. Document the core value proposition of zero-dependency, in-browser rendering with automatic fallback capabilities. Describe the target audience including developers, technical writers, and AI agent users. Include the key benefits such as no build tools, bilingual support, and reusable template system. Provide a high-level architecture overview showing the relationship between frontend rendering, server fallback, and AI integration. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: CTU File Format
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the CTU (Code-To-UML Template) file format that defines structured data for diagram examples. Document the complete syntax including header sections (Title, Describe), example block structure with metadata fields, PlantUML source code formatting, and localization support. Explain the hierarchical organization of CTU files, how examples are categorized and grouped, and the relationship between different CTU files. Detail the parsing mechanism used by the application to extract diagram data from CTU files. Include practical examples showing various CTU file structures, best practices for organizing diagram examples, and guidelines for maintaining consistency across template files. Address the role of CTU files in the overall template system and their integration with HTML presentation templates.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system design documentation for Code-To-UML. Describe the overall system architecture showing the browser-first approach with PlantUML WASM as primary renderer and Java-based fallback. Document the separation of concerns between data (CTU files), presentation (HTML templates), and logic (JavaScript). Explain the dual-mode operation supporting both local development and production environments. Detail the technology stack including JavaScript ES6+, HTML5, CSS3, PlantUML, and Node.js. Include system boundaries, external dependencies, and integration points. Address the modular component architecture and how different subsystems interact. Provide architectural diagrams showing system components and their relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: UML Diagrams
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for UML diagram examples in Code-To-UML. Cover all nine standard UML diagram types: Class diagrams, Sequence diagrams, Use Case diagrams, Object diagrams, Activity diagrams, Component diagrams, Deployment diagrams, State diagrams, and Timing diagrams. Document the numbering system (class--1_en.ctu, sequence--1_zh.ctu) and explain how examples progress from basic to advanced complexity. Include PlantUML syntax patterns for each diagram type, common use cases, and best practices. Provide guidance on when to use each diagram type and how they complement software architecture documentation. Address the bilingual nature of examples and explain the relationship between English and Chinese variants.

wiki_catalogs.name: Main Application Controller
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the main application controller (demo.js). Explain its role as the central orchestrator managing UI interactions, rendering pipeline, and component coordination. Document the initialization process, tab switching mechanisms, diagram loading workflows, and internationalization handling. Detail the render queue management system, error handling strategies, and fallback mechanisms. Include the language switching system, preview lightbox functionality, and table of contents synchronization. Provide examples of the controller's event handling patterns, promise-based rendering chain, and component integration through global window objects. Address performance optimizations, memory management, and cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Skill Definition System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Code-To-UML skill definition system. Explain the SKILL.md structure and YAML-based skill configuration format. Document the purpose and hard rules defined in the skill specification, including template reuse requirements, scope constraints, and output formatting standards. Detail the workflow stages from scope resolution to final verification. Explain the mandatory report sections and their content requirements for different analysis scopes (project, module, file, class, function). Document the UML standards and quality bar requirements. Include examples of proper skill configuration and common pitfalls to avoid. Address the verification checklist and final response format requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server REST Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Code-To-UML's server-side REST endpoints. Document the GET /api/demo-examples endpoint including query parameters (lang: language filter, dir: data directory selection) and response format containing diagram examples grouped by type. Detail the POST /api/plantuml-svg endpoint for server-side rendering fallback with request body specification (source: PlantUML code string) and SVG response format. Document cache management endpoints including GET /api/cache-html for listing generated HTML files, DELETE /api/cache-html for removing specific files, and DELETE /api/cache-html/all for clearing all generated content. Include HTTP status codes, error responses, request/response schemas, and practical examples with curl commands. Address security considerations, input validation, and rate limiting implications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cursor Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed setup documentation for Cursor AI integration with Code-To-UML. Document the CTU_HOME environment variable configuration and project registration process specific to Cursor. Provide step-by-step installation and configuration instructions including Cursor workspace setup, skill activation, and credential configuration. Explain Cursor-specific YAML settings and agent configuration options. Include troubleshooting common Cursor integration issues such as authentication problems, skill loading failures, and rendering performance optimization. Document Cursor workspace best practices and project structure requirements for optimal Code-To-UML functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Code-To-UML. Cover prerequisites including Node.js 18+ and Java requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions starting from repository cloning through server setup. Document the optional CTU_HOME environment variable configuration for AI agent integration. Include detailed server startup procedures for different platforms (Windows batch script, Unix shell script, direct Node.js execution). Explain how to access the demo interface and verify successful installation. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for prerequisite software installation. Make the content accessible to beginners while ensuring all necessary technical details are covered.

wiki_catalogs.name: HTML Template System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the HTML template system that transforms CTU data into interactive web pages. Document the cache/_TEMPLATE.html structure as the base template for generated reports, explaining the placeholder system, content insertion mechanisms, and layout organization. Detail the CSS custom property system used for theming and styling customization, including color schemes, typography, and responsive design patterns. Explain the component integration system that connects UI components with template rendering. Document the dynamic content injection process, how CTU data maps to HTML elements, and the relationship between template variables and rendered output. Include examples of template customization, styling modifications, and component integration patterns. Address the rendering pipeline from CTU data to final HTML output, error handling during template processing, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rendering Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the rendering pipeline architecture. Thoroughly explain the two-tier rendering strategy with PlantUML WASM as the primary renderer and automatic fallback to server-side Java-based rendering. Document the rendering pipeline stages including source validation, WASM execution, SVG generation, and error handling. Detail the failure detection mechanism and recovery strategies for rendering failures. Explain the buffer management for failed renders and the retry logic. Include the integration with markdown-it for description processing and the component-based rendering system. Address performance optimizations, memory management, and large diagram handling. Provide concrete examples of the rendering workflow from CTU source to final HTML output.

wiki_catalogs.name: Non-UML Diagrams
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Non-UML diagram examples including Gantt charts, Mind Maps, Work Breakdown Structures, EBNF grammars, Regular Expressions, Network diagrams, JSON/YAML structures, Archimate diagrams, NWdiag network visualizations, Salt orchestration diagrams, and Wireframe mockups. Explain the purpose and syntax of each diagram type, their specific use cases in software development and documentation, and how they complement traditional UML diagrams. Document the PlantUML syntax variations for non-standard diagrams and provide practical examples showing real-world applications. Address the integration with the broader Code-To-UML ecosystem and explain when to choose each diagram type for different documentation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Documentation Page Core
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the docs-page-core.js component, which provides essential rendering utilities and DOM manipulation functions. Document the core rendering functions, example source reading mechanisms, preview ID management, and message handling systems. Explain the utility functions for example manipulation, DOM traversal, and state management. Include examples of how this component integrates with the main controller and other UI components. Detail the error messaging system, example validation, and rendering state tracking. Address the component's role in the overall architecture and its dependency relationships with other core modules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Setup Guides
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive setup guides for all supported AI agents in Code-To-UML. Document the CTU_HOME environment variable configuration and project registration process. Provide step-by-step setup instructions for Cursor, Claude Code, Qwen Coder, and OpenAI Codex agents. Detail the agent-specific configuration files and YAML settings. Explain the differences between each agent type and their unique setup requirements. Include troubleshooting common setup issues and validation steps. Document the agent selection process and best practices for optimal performance. Address security considerations and credential management for each agent type.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend JavaScript APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Code-To-UML's frontend JavaScript APIs and component interfaces. Document the main application controller functions in demo.js including initialization, rendering pipeline orchestration, and event handling mechanisms. Detail component interfaces from docs-page-core.js for rendering utilities, error handling, and DOM manipulation. Explain the table of contents component API from toc-component.js including navigation generation, scroll synchronization, and interactive features. Document the demo example component interface from demo-example-component.js covering individual diagram card rendering, click handlers, and lightbox integration. Include method signatures, parameter specifications, return values, and usage examples. Address component lifecycle, event propagation, and integration patterns between different frontend modules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Claude Code Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive setup documentation for Claude Code AI integration. Document the Claude Code API key configuration and project registration process. Provide detailed step-by-step instructions for Claude Code workspace setup, skill configuration, and authentication. Explain Claude Code-specific settings in the YAML configuration file and environment variable requirements. Include Claude Code workspace best practices, model selection recommendations, and performance tuning. Address common Claude Code setup issues such as API key validation, rate limiting, and workspace permissions. Document Claude Code's unique features and limitations compared to other agents.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for Code-To-UML's system design. Describe the high-level architecture showing the browser-first rendering pipeline with PlantUML WASM as the primary renderer and automatic fallback to server-side Java-based rendering. Document the component-based frontend architecture with modular UI components. Explain the template system that separates data (CTU files), presentation (HTML templates), and logic (JavaScript). Detail the internationalization system using localStorage persistence and event-driven language switching. Include the server infrastructure built around a lightweight Node.js development server providing API endpoints. Address the two-tier rendering strategy, error handling mechanisms, and the relationship between cache management and report generation. Provide system context diagrams showing component interactions and data flows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the component-based architecture. Describe the modular component system including docs-page-core.js for core rendering utilities, toc-component.js for table of contents generation, demo-example-component.js for individual example cards, and render-failure-common.js for error handling. Document component interfaces, event-driven communication, and data flow between components. Explain the factory pattern used for component creation and the observer pattern for language switching. Detail component lifecycle management, DOM manipulation strategies, and responsive design implementation. Include component composition patterns and how components interact to create the final user interface. Address component reusability, customization options, and extension points.

wiki_catalogs.name: Official PlantUML Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the official PlantUML demo integration in Code-To-UML. Explain how the plantuml-official-demo directory provides standardized examples from the official PlantUML documentation, organized by language (en/zh). Document the relationship between these official examples and the custom CTU examples in the data/demo directory. Explain how the integration enhances the example library with authoritative PlantUML syntax patterns and best practices. Include guidance on comparing official examples with Code-To-UML variants, understanding syntax differences, and leveraging both sources for comprehensive learning. Address the bilingual nature of the official demos and explain how they support international users.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table of Contents Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the toc-component.js module responsible for generating and managing the side table of contents. Document the TOC rendering system, active state synchronization, and viewport-based navigation. Explain the component's integration with the main application controller, scroll event handling, and dynamic content updates. Include examples of TOC item creation, event binding, and navigation functionality. Detail the viewport line detection algorithm, active link highlighting, and smooth scrolling integration. Address performance considerations for large document structures and responsive behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workflow Automation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the automated workflow from code analysis to report generation in Code-To-UML. Explain the integration between AI agents and the Code-To-UML pipeline. Detail the CTU_HOME project initialization process and environment setup. Document the automated report generation workflow including scope resolution, template reading, code analysis, data file generation, and HTML report creation. Explain the server startup and verification process. Include examples of automated workflows for different analysis scopes. Address error handling and recovery mechanisms in the automated process. Document best practices for optimizing automated report generation performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Qwen Coder Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed setup documentation for Qwen Coder AI integration with Code-To-UML. Document the Qwen API configuration, authentication setup, and project registration process. Provide step-by-step instructions for Qwen workspace configuration, skill activation, and credential management. Explain Qwen-specific YAML settings and environment variable configuration. Include Qwen workspace best practices, model selection, and performance optimization techniques. Address common Qwen setup issues such as API key configuration, regional availability, and workspace permissions. Document Qwen's unique capabilities and integration patterns specific to Code-To-UML workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Code-To-UML's core component system. Document the main application controller (demo.js) and its role in orchestrating UI interactions and rendering pipeline. Explain the component architecture including reusable UI components like docs-page-core.js for rendering utilities, toc-component.js for navigation, demo-example-component.js for individual diagram cards, and render-failure-common.js for error handling. Detail the component interfaces, event systems, and data flow patterns. Include component lifecycle management, state handling, and integration patterns. Provide examples of component usage and customization options. Address the modular design philosophy and how components communicate through well-defined interfaces.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for the template architecture system. Explain the dual-template approach with CTU files defining data structure and HTML templates providing presentation layer. Document the template parsing system that converts CTU files into JSON payloads for frontend consumption. Detail the HTML template structure in _TEMPLATE.html and how it integrates with the component system. Explain the CSS custom properties system and theming capabilities. Address template inheritance patterns, localization support, and dynamic content injection. Include the relationship between template files and generated HTML reports. Document template customization options, validation mechanisms, and best practices for extending the template system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Example Management and Organization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for managing and organizing diagram examples in Code-To-UML. Explain the CTU file naming convention (category--number_language.ctu), the directory structure in data/demo/, and how examples are loaded and displayed in the demo interface. Document the bilingual organization system with English (_en) and Chinese (_zh) variants, explaining how language selection affects example loading and display. Provide guidance on adding new examples, modifying existing ones, maintaining consistency across language variants, and organizing examples by complexity and use case. Include best practices for example authoring, syntax validation, and performance optimization for large diagrams.

wiki_catalogs.name: Demo Example Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the demo-example-component.js module that handles individual diagram example rendering and interaction. Document the example card creation system, source input handling, action button management, and preview generation. Explain the component's integration with the rendering pipeline, event delegation patterns, and user interaction handling. Include examples of the createExampleNode function, markdown rendering capabilities, and example localization. Detail the component's state management, DOM manipulation patterns, and integration with the main application controller. Address the component's role in the overall demo page architecture and its reusability patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: OpenAI Codex Support
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive setup documentation for OpenAI Codex integration with Code-To-UML. Document the OpenAI API key configuration, project registration, and environment variable setup. Provide detailed step-by-step instructions for OpenAI workspace configuration, skill activation, and authentication process. Explain OpenAI-specific YAML settings and configuration options in the agent setup file. Include OpenAI workspace best practices, model selection recommendations, and cost optimization strategies. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Code-To-UML's template system that enables reusable report generation. Document the CTU file format structure including headers (Title, Describe), example blocks with metadata, and PlantUML source code sections. Explain the HTML template system using cache/_TEMPLATE.html as the base for generated reports. Detail the relationship between CTU data files and HTML presentation templates. Include the template inheritance pattern, variable substitution mechanisms, and content organization strategies. Document the CSS custom property system for theming and styling customization. Provide examples of template creation, modification, and best practices for maintaining consistency across generated reports. Address localization support and how templates handle bilingual content.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the server infrastructure built around the Node.js development server. Document the API endpoints including /api/demo-examples for loading diagram examples, /api/plantuml-svg for server-side rendering, and cache management endpoints. Explain the file management system for CTU files and generated HTML reports. Detail the cross-platform server setup with shell scripts and batch files for Windows compatibility. Address the development server configuration, port management, and static file serving. Include the relationship between server-side operations and client-side rendering. Document server deployment considerations, environment configuration, and maintenance procedures. Provide examples of server API usage and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Render Failure Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the render-failure-common.js module that provides comprehensive error handling and recovery mechanisms for diagram rendering. Document the failure buffer system, error detection algorithms, and automatic retry mechanisms. Explain the render timeout handling, unknown state detection, and fallback strategies. Include examples of the renderWithFailureHandling function, error categorization, and user feedback systems. Detail the component's integration with the rendering pipeline, error recovery workflows, and diagnostic capabilities. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create a comparative guide for selecting the optimal AI agent for Code-To-UML projects. Compare Cursor, Claude Code, Qwen Coder, and OpenAI Codex agents across key criteria including cost, performance, accuracy, and feature support. Document the decision matrix for choosing agents based on project requirements, budget constraints, and technical specifications. Explain agent-specific strengths and limitations for different use cases such as code analysis, documentation generation, and report automation. Include migration strategies between agents and best practices for agent configuration management. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Code-To-UML's extensive diagram example library. Organize examples by UML categories (Sequence, Use Case, Class, Object, Activity, Component, Deployment, State, Timing) and Non-UML categories (Gantt, MindMap, WBS, EBNF, Regex, Network, JSON, YAML, Archimate, Salt). Document the naming conventions for CTU files and how examples are organized within the data/demo/ directory structure. Explain the bilingual nature of examples with English and Chinese variants. Include the official PlantUML demo integration and how it complements the built-in examples. Provide guidance on adding new examples, modifying existing ones, and maintaining consistency across the example library. Address the relationship between example complexity and rendering performance.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Code-To-UML's internationalization system. Explain the event-driven language switching system with the 'docs:langchange' event and how components respond to language changes. Detail the translation management structure with separate language files for English and Chinese. Include the implementation of dynamic content switching and how the system maintains consistency across all UI elements. Document best practices for adding new languages and extending the translation system. Address the relationship between language preferences and cached content. Provide examples of implementing multilingual support in custom components and templates.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Agent Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Code-To-UML's AI agent integration capabilities. Document the SKILL.md structure and how it enables AI coding assistants to generate analysis reports automatically. Explain the supported agents including Cursor, Claude Code, Qwen Coder, and OpenAI Codex. Detail the CTU_HOME environment variable setup and project registration process. Document the agent configuration files and YAML-based skill definitions. Include step-by-step guides for each supported agent showing setup procedures and usage patterns. Explain the workflow from code analysis to automated report generation. Address troubleshooting common integration issues and best practices for optimizing AI-assisted report generation. Provide examples of agent-specific configurations and customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Code-To-UML's REST endpoints and frontend APIs. Document the GET /api/demo-examples endpoint including query parameters (lang filtering) and response format. Detail the POST /api/plantuml-svg endpoint for server-side rendering fallback with request/response specifications. Document cache management endpoints including listing and clearing generated HTML files. Explain the frontend API exposed through demo.js including component interfaces and event systems. Include authentication requirements (none for local development), rate limiting considerations, and error response formats. Provide practical examples of API usage with curl commands and JavaScript fetch examples. Address CORS configuration for development environments and security considerations for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling and Theming
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Code-To-UML's styling and theming system. Document the CSS custom property system used for theming and how it enables easy customization of colors, fonts, and layout. Explain the component styling architecture and how CSS variables cascade through the component hierarchy. Detail the responsive design implementation and mobile-first approach. Include guidelines for creating custom themes by modifying CSS variables and overriding component styles. Document the relationship between global styles in main.css and component-specific styling. Provide examples of common customization scenarios such as brand color changes, typography adjustments, and layout modifications. Address browser compatibility considerations and performance implications of styling choices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing and Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Code-To-UML's testing framework and quality assurance processes. Document the test suite structure with unit tests for various components including cache HTML API, demo tabs, markdown rendering, and layout validation. Explain the testing methodology using Node.js test runner and how tests validate both frontend functionality and server-side APIs. Detail the test coverage areas including component rendering, API endpoints, internationalization, and error handling scenarios. Include guidelines for writing new tests and adding test cases for custom components. Document continuous integration considerations and quality gates. Provide examples of testing patterns and best practices for maintaining test reliability across different environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Production
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for deploying Code-To-UML in production environments. Document server configuration requirements including Node.js runtime setup, Java installation for fallback rendering, and environment variable configuration. Explain the production deployment topology with static file serving and API endpoint exposure. Detail security considerations including CORS configuration, input validation for PlantUML rendering, and protection against malicious input. Document performance optimization strategies including caching mechanisms, CDN integration, and load balancing considerations. Include containerization options using Docker and cloud platform deployment guides. Address monitoring and logging requirements, backup strategies for generated reports, and maintenance procedures for production systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation for Code-To-UML addressing common issues and their solutions. Document PlantUML rendering problems including WASM compatibility issues, large diagram limitations, and Java dependency problems. Explain error categorization and recovery mechanisms implemented in render-failure-common.js. Address browser compatibility issues, JavaScript runtime errors, and component rendering failures. Include diagnostic procedures for identifying root causes and systematic approaches to problem resolution. Document frequently asked questions about installation, configuration, usage patterns, and performance optimization. Provide step-by-step solutions for common scenarios including missing dependencies, permission issues, and network connectivity problems. Include logging strategies and debugging techniques for advanced troubleshooting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing and Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for contributing to Code-To-UML development. Document the development environment setup including Git workflow, branch management, and local development procedures. Explain code standards and conventions for JavaScript, CSS, and HTML development. Detail the testing requirements and how to add new tests for contributed features. Document the pull request process, code review guidelines, and merge criteria. Include guidelines for adding new diagram examples, extending the template system, and developing new UI components. Address documentation standards and how to update README files and inline comments. Provide examples of common contribution scenarios including bug fixes, feature additions, and performance improvements. Document the release process and versioning strategy.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Code-To-UML: Interactive UML Report Generator

## Project Introduction

Code-To-UML is a browser-first UML documentation tool that transforms code analysis reports into interactive HTML documents with 100+ diagram examples. The project provides a zero-dependency, in-browser solution for generating comprehensive UML documentation without requiring build tools or server-side processing.

### Purpose Statement
- **Primary Goal**: Convert source code analysis into interactive UML reports with bilingual support
- **Core Mission**: Eliminate reliance on heavyweight IDEs, fragile build pipelines, or opaque SaaS tools
- **Target Audience**: Developers, architects, and technical writers who need comprehensive code documentation

### Key Objectives
- Browser-first rendering using PlantUML WASM for zero server latency
- Automatic fallback to server-side rendering when WASM limitations are encountered
- Bilingual interface with persistent language preferences
- Interactive lightbox for full-screen diagram exploration
- Reusable template system for consistent report generation

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a client-server architecture with a focus on browser-based rendering:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[PlantUML WASM Renderer]
    A --> C[Local Storage]
    B --> D[Interactive UI]
    C --> D
    D --> E[Template System]
    E --> F[Generated Reports]
    
    G[Node.js Server] --> H[PlantUML JAR Fallback]
    G --> I[Static File Serving]
    G --> J[API Endpoints]
    
    H --> K[SVG Generation]
    I --> L[File System]
    J --> M[Cache Management]
    
    K --> N[Server Response]
    L --> O[Report Files]
    M --> P[HTML Index]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**:
- **Rendering Engine**: PlantUML WASM for client-side diagram generation
- **UI Components**: Modular JavaScript components for demos, examples, and navigation
- **Styling System**: CSS custom properties for theming and responsive design
- **Internationalization**: Custom i18n system with localStorage persistence

**Backend Layer**:
- **Development Server**: Node.js HTTP server with API endpoints
- **File Management**: Cache system for generated HTML reports
- **Template Processing**: CTU data file parser and renderer
- **Fallback System**: Java-based PlantUML JAR for edge cases

### Design Patterns

**Template Pattern**: The project uses a template-based approach where `.ctu` files define report structure and content, while HTML templates provide presentation layer.

**Observer Pattern**: Event-driven architecture for language switching and UI updates through `docs:langchange` events.

**Factory Pattern**: Component creation through factory functions that generate reusable UI elements.

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Primary Pages**:
- `demo.html` - Interactive diagram showcase with 20+ diagram types
- `index.html` - Cache index for generated reports
- `serve.js` - Development server with API endpoints

**Core Modules**:
- `demo.js` - Main page controller and rendering logic
- `component/docs-page-core.js` - Core rendering utilities and error handling
- `component/toc-component.js` - Table of contents generator
- `component/demo-example-component.js` - Individual example card renderer
- `i18n-config.js` - Internationalization system

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**:
- Port settings via `PORT` environment variable or CLI arguments
- Project root via `CTU_HOME` environment variable
- Language preferences stored in localStorage

**Template System**:
- `data/_TEMPLATE.ctu` - Defines structure for report sections
- `cache/_TEMPLATE.html` - Provides HTML template for generated reports
- Diagram categories: overview, structure, objects, architecture, flow, calls, dataflow, code, principles, guide

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies**:
- **PlantUML**: Diagram rendering engine (WASM and JAR versions)
- **Viz.js**: Graph layout engine for non-UML diagrams
- **markdown-it**: Markdown processing for descriptions
- **Node.js**: Development server runtime

**Browser Libraries**:
- `plantuml.js` - WASM-based PlantUML renderer
- `viz-global.js` - Graphviz layout engine
- `markdown-it.js` - Markdown processing
- `openiconic.js` - Icon library

### Integration Points

**API Endpoints**:
- `/api/demo-examples` - Load diagram examples for the demo page
- `/api/plantuml-svg` - Server-side PlantUML rendering fallback
- `/api/cache-html` - List generated HTML files
- `/api/cache-html/all` - Clear generated cache files

**File System Integration**:
- `data/` directory for CTU template files
- `cache/` directory for generated HTML reports
- `component/` directory for reusable UI components

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[demo.html] --> B[demo.js]
    B --> C[docs-page-core.js]
    B --> D[toc-component.js]
    B --> E[demo-example-component.js]
    B --> F[i18n-config.js]
    
    G[serve.js] --> H[CTU Parser]
    G --> I[Cache Manager]
    G --> J[File System]
    
    C --> K[PlantUML Renderer]
    D --> L[Table of Contents]
    E --> M[Example Cards]
    F --> N[Language Switcher]
    
    H --> O[Data Directory]
    I --> P[Cache Directory]
    J --> Q[Static Assets]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Interactive Diagram Rendering**:
- Browser-first rendering using PlantUML WASM
- Automatic fallback to server-side rendering
- Support for 20+ diagram types including UML and non-UML formats
- Real-time editing and preview capabilities

**Bilingual Interface**:
- English and Chinese language support
- Persistent language preferences
- Dynamic content switching without page reload
- Customizable language toggle

**Report Generation**:
- Template-based report creation system
- Automated HTML report generation
- Cache management for generated content
- API-driven content loading

### Implementation Highlights

**Rendering Pipeline**:
- Two-tier rendering strategy for maximum reliability
- Error detection and categorization
- Automatic retry logic for failed renders
- Large diagram scaling support

**UI Components**:
- Modular component architecture
- Responsive design with mobile support
- Interactive lightbox for diagram viewing
- Scroll-synced table of contents

**Data Management**:
- Structured CTU file format
- Hierarchical data organization
- Automatic example grouping
- Multilingual content support

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> Rendering: Diagram Source Available
    Loading --> Error: Missing Source
    
    Rendering --> Success: WASM Render OK
    Rendering --> Fallback: WASM Failed
    Rendering --> LargeDiagram: Large Diagram Detected
    
    Fallback --> Success: JAR Render OK
    Fallback --> Error: JAR Failed
    
    LargeDiagram --> Success: Scaled Render OK
    LargeDiagram --> Error: Scale Failed
    
    Success --> Interactive: Enable Controls
    Error --> ErrorDisplay: Show Error Message
    
    Interactive --> Editing: User Edits Source
    Editing --> Rendering: Source Changed
```

**Performance Considerations**:
- Lazy loading of components and assets
- Efficient DOM manipulation and event handling
- Memory management for large diagram rendering
- Optimized SVG generation and caching

**Extensibility Features**:
- Plugin architecture for new diagram types
- Template customization system
- Custom styling through CSS variables
- Extensible component system

---

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [demo.html](demo.html)
- [demo.js](demo.js)
- [serve.js](serve.js)
- [main.css](main.css)
- [component/docs-page-core.js](component/docs-page-core.js)
- [component/toc-component.js](component/toc-component.js)
- [component/demo-example-component.js](component/demo-example-component.js)
- [i18n-config.js](i18n-config.js)
- [data/_TEMPLATE.ctu](data/_TEMPLATE.ctu)
- [skills/code-to-uml/SKILL.md](skills/code-to-uml/SKILL.md)

</blog>
